guidelines: Register as a voter using Form 6 on the National Voter Service Portal (NVSP).
Carry your EPIC (Voter ID Card) or any ECI-approved photo ID to the polling booth.
Check your name in the electoral roll on the CEO Maharashtra website before election day.
Polling hours are typically 7:00 AM to 6:00 PM.
NOTA (None of the Above) is available as an option on the EVM.
Model Code of Conduct is enforced from the date of election announcement.
Voter registration is open year-round via Form 6 (online or offline).
You must be 18+ years old on the qualifying date (Jan 1 of the revision year).
Carry EPIC or any of the 12 ECI-approved alternative photo IDs.
EVMs and VVPATs are used in all polling stations.
You can check your polling booth location on the CEO Karnataka website.
Webcasting is done at select booths for transparency.
Delhi has 70 Vidhan Sabha constituencies and 7 Lok Sabha constituencies.
Register online via NVSP or the Voter Helpline App.
EPIC or Aadhaar, PAN, Passport, or Driving License accepted as ID at the booth.
Polling hours in Delhi are typically 8:00 AM to 6:00 PM.
Special arrangements are made for senior citizens and Persons with Disabilities (PwD).
Check your name in the electoral roll using the Voter Helpline App or SMS to 1950.
Tamil Nadu has 234 Assembly constituencies and 39 Lok Sabha constituencies.
Register via Form 6 on NVSP or at your nearest ERO office.
Voter ID (EPIC) is the primary identification document at the polling booth.
Polling hours are generally 7:00 AM to 6:00 PM.
Use the cVIGIL app to report Model Code of Conduct violations.
Accessible polling booths are available for PwD voters.
UP is India's largest state by Lok Sabha seats (80 constituencies).
Elections in UP are typically held in multiple phases due to the state's size.
Register online at NVSP or through the Voter Helpline App (available in Hindi).
Carry EPIC or Aadhaar / any ECI-approved photo ID to the polling booth.
Each phase has a separate campaign silence period (48 hours before polling).
Indelible ink is applied to the left index finger after voting.
West Bengal has 294 Assembly seats and 42 Lok Sabha constituencies.
Register via NVSP or visit your Block Level Officer (BLO).
Voter ID card or 11 other approved photo IDs accepted.
Polling booths open from 7:00 AM to 5:00 PM / 6:00 PM depending on the phase.
Central Armed Police Forces (CAPF) are deployed for election security.
Use the Voter Helpline number 1950 for any election-related queries.
Kerala has 140 Assembly seats and 20 Lok Sabha constituencies.
Kerala typically records one of the highest voter turnouts in India.
Register via NVSP portal or the Voter Helpline App.
Carry your EPIC or Aadhaar card to the polling booth.
Polling hours are 7:00 AM to 6:00 PM.
Postal ballot facility available for service voters, PwD, and senior citizens (80+).
Gujarat has 182 Assembly seats and 26 Lok Sabha constituencies.
Register online at NVSP or through the Voter Helpline App.
EPIC is the primary voter ID — alternatives include Aadhaar, passport, DL.
Voting is done on EVMs with VVPAT verification.
Polling hours are typically 8:00 AM to 6:00 PM.
Booth Level Officers (BLOs) conduct door-to-door voter verification drives.
